title: Trade Into a Winning Endgame
setup: Two rooks face each other on the **a-file** — and Black's king is also on the back rank.
hint: When you can trade rooks with check and emerge into a winning endgame, do it without hesitation.
explanation:
## The Idea

This is a **simplification** tactic. White's rook trades with Black's rook, but with a critical bonus: it does so **with check**, dictating Black's response.

White plays {{Rxa8+}}.

## Black's Forced Reply

The rook on a8 attacks the king on e8 along the **eighth rank**. Black has only one option:

- {{Kxa8}} — impossible, king cannot teleport four squares.
- The king must move out of check: {{Kd7}}, {{Ke7}}, or {{Kf7}}. Each leaves the rook on a8 alive.

Wait — actually with the rook now on a8 and undefended, black's king can never reach it (4 squares away). Black moves the king, **and White is up an entire rook!**

## Why This Wins

The resulting position is **King + Rook vs. lone King** — a forced win. Every beginner should know how to deliver mate with K+R vs K. This puzzle is therefore a forced win in the cleanest possible way: a single capture leads directly to a winning endgame.

## Pattern to Memorize

> A trade that brings you into a winning endgame is worth far more than a flashy attack that fizzles.

Look for opportunities to **simplify** when you are ahead, even by tactical means.
